title: Phototaxis suppression
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5pvon","text":"To suppress both positive and negative phototactic behavior, we use an aversive stimulus. We place a filter paper wetted with either a 0.15M quinine/water solution (gustatory stimulus) or an 0.1 mixture (v/v) of benzaldehyde/paraffin oil (olfactory stimulus) in the preferred tube, i.e., the transparent or the opaque tube, depending on the flight status of the flies and hence their phototaxis (positive or negative, respectively).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
creator: Björn Brembs
